metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_4650
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 5b,e. ImmunoEM shows localization of GluRε3 (Glutamate receptor subunit epsilon-3, GRIN2C) to postsynaptic densities forming synaptic contacts with mossy fibre terminals in the cerebellar granule layer using immunoperoxidase (b) and postembedding immunogold (e) labeling.

"integral component of ... membrane" term was selected based on protein's known transmembrane topology. Experimental description: "The brains of C57BL/6J, GluRε1 homozygote (-/-) (Sakimura et al., 1995), and GluRε3 (-/-) (M. Abe, M. Fukaya, M. Watanabe and K. Sakimura, unpublished observation) mice were used for immunoblot and immunohistochemical analyses."

"In the present study, we raised polyclonal antibodies to mouse NMDA receptor subunits GluRε3 (GluRε3C antibody) and GluRζ1 (GluRζ1C antibody). Two peptides DVSGSLDRATRTIENWGNNR (single amino acid code) and LQNQKDTVLPRRAIEREEGQLQLCSRHRES were synthesized; the former corresponds to the intracellular domain downstream of the last transmembrane domain (M4) of GluRε3 [amino acid residues 908-927; Kutsuwada et al., 1992; (E06595, Genebank)], while the latter corresponds to the C-terminus of the C2 cassette of GluRζ1 (amino acid residues 909-938; Yamazaki et al., 1992; D10028)."

note that the antibodies were validated in Figures 1 and 3.
